ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏حَبِيبٍ يَعْنِي ابْنَ الشَّهِيدِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي مُلَيْكَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏شَهِدْتُ ‏ ‏ابْنَ الزُّبَيْرِ ‏ ‏وَابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ ‏ ‏ابْنُ الزُّبَيْرِ ‏ ‏لِابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏أَتَذْكُرُ حِينَ اسْتَقْبَلْنَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَقَدْ جَاءَ مِنْ سَفَرٍ فَقَالَ نَعَمْ ‏ ‏فَحَمَلَنِي وَفُلَانًا ‏ ‏غُلَامًا مِنْ ‏ ‏بَنِي هَاشِمٍ ‏ ‏وَتَرَكَكَ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, Shu'bah narrated to us, from Habib, meaning Ibn al-Shahid, from 'Abdullah ibn Abi Mulaykah, who said: I witnessed Ibn al-Zubayr and Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهم). Ibn al-Zubayr said to Ibn 'Abbas: "Do you remember when we went to meet the Messenger of Allah ﷺ when he returned from a journey?" He said: "Yes, he carried me and so-and-so, a boy from Banu Hashim, and left you."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سِمَاكِ بْنِ حَرْبٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَدْخُلُ عَلَيْكُمْ رَجُلٌ يَنْظُرُ بِعَيْنِ شَيْطَانٍ ‏ ‏أَوْ بِعَيْنَيْ شَيْطَانٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ فَدَخَلَ رَجُلٌ أَزْرَقُ فَقَالَ يَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ ‏ ‏عَلَامَ سَبَبْتَنِي ‏ ‏أَوْ شَتَمْتَنِي أَوْ نَحْوَ هَذَا ‏ ‏قَالَ وَجَعَلَ يَحْلِفُ قَالَ فَنَزَلَتْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ فِي الْمُجَادَلَةِ ‏ { ‏وَيَحْلِفُونَ عَلَى الْكَذِبِ وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ ‏} ‏وَالْآيَةُ الْأُخْرَى ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, Shu'bah narrated to us, from Simak ibn Harb, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "A man will enter upon you who looks with the eye of a Shaytan" — or "with the eyes of a Shaytan." He said: A blue-eyed man entered and said: "O Muhammad, why did you revile me" — or "insult me" — or something to that effect. He said: He began swearing oaths. He said: Then this verse was revealed in Surah al-Mujadilah: {And they swear upon a lie while they know} [58:14], and the other verse.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سِمَاكِ بْنِ حَرْبٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ فِي ‏ ‏الدَّجَّالِ ‏ ‏أَعْوَرُ ‏ ‏هِجَانٌ ‏ ‏أَزْهَرُ ‏ ‏كَأَنَّ رَأْسَهُ ‏ ‏أَصَلَةٌ ‏ ‏أَشْبَهُ النَّاسِ ‏ ‏بِعَبْدِ الْعُزَّى بْنِ قَطَنٍ ‏ ‏فَإِمَّا هَلَكَ الْهُلَّكُ فَإِنَّ رَبَّكُمْ تَعَالَى لَيْسَ بِأَعْوَرَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏فَحَدَّثْتُ ‏ ‏بِهِ ‏ ‏قَتَادَةَ ‏ ‏فَحَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏بِنَحْوٍ مِنْ هَذَا ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, Shu'bah narrated to us, from Simak ibn Harb, from 'Ikrimah, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), from the Prophet ﷺ, that he said regarding the Dajjal: "One-eyed, white-skinned, bright-complexioned, as if his head were a serpent (asalah), the most resembling of people to 'Abd al-'Uzza ibn Qatan. If the doomed are to perish, then indeed your Lord Most High is not one-eyed." Shu'bah said: I narrated this to Qatadah, and he narrated to me something similar.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُعَاذُ بْنُ هِشَامٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏قَتَادَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَجُلًا أَتَى النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ يَا نَبِيَّ اللَّهِ إِنِّي شَيْخٌ كَبِيرٌ عَلِيلٌ يَشُقُّ عَلَيَّ ‏ ‏الْقِيَامُ ‏ ‏فَأْمُرْنِي بِلَيْلَةٍ لَعَلَّ اللَّهَ يُوَفِّقُنِي فِيهَا ‏ ‏لِلَيْلَةِ ‏ ‏الْقَدْرِ قَالَ عَلَيْكَ بِالسَّابِعَةِ ‏
en: Mu'adh ibn Hisham narrated to us, my father narrated to me, from Qatadah, from 'Ikrimah, from 'Abdullah ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), that a man came to the Prophet ﷺ and said: "O Prophet of Allah, I am an old, sick man, and standing in prayer is difficult for me. So direct me to a night so that perhaps Allah will grant me success in catching Laylat al-Qadr." He said: "You should observe the seventh (of the last ten nights of Ramadan)."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي حَمْزَةَ ‏ ‏سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏مَرَّ بِي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَأَنَا أَلْعَبُ مَعَ ‏ ‏الْغِلْمَانِ فَاخْتَبَأْتُ مِنْهُ خَلْفَ بَابٍ ‏ ‏فَدَعَانِي ‏ ‏فَحَطَأَنِي ‏ ‏حَطْأَةً ‏ ‏ثُمَّ بَعَثَ بِي إِلَى ‏ ‏مُعَاوِيَةَ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, Shu'bah narrated to us, from Abu Hamzah, who said: I heard Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما) saying: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ passed by me while I was playing with the boys. I hid from him behind a door. He called me and patted me on the back, then sent me to Mu'awiyah.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي بِشْرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَصُومُ حَتَّى نَقُولَ لَا يُرِيدُ أَنْ يُفْطِرَ وَيُفْطِرُ حَتَّى نَقُولَ لَا يُرِيدُ أَنْ يَصُومَ وَمَا صَامَ شَهْرًا مُتَتَابِعًا غَيْرَ رَمَضَانَ مُنْذُ قَدِمَ ‏ ‏الْمَدِينَةَ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to me, Shu'bah narrated to us, from Abu Bishr, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to fast until we would say: "He does not intend to break his fast," and he would break his fast until we would say: "He does not intend to fast." He never fasted a complete consecutive month other than Ramadan since he came to Madinah.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هُشَيْمٌ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ بْنُ أَبِي زِيَادٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏مُجَاهِدٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏أَهَلَّ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏بِالْحَجِّ فَلَمَّا قَدِمَ طَافَ ‏ ‏بِالْبَيْتِ ‏ ‏وَبَيْنَ ‏ ‏الصَّفَا ‏ ‏وَالْمَرْوَةِ ‏ ‏وَلَمْ يُقَصِّرْ وَلَمْ يُحِلَّ مِنْ أَجْلِ ‏ ‏الْهَدْيِ ‏ ‏وَأَمَرَ مَنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ سَاقَ ‏ ‏الْهَدْيَ ‏ ‏أَنْ يَطُوفَ وَأَنْ ‏ ‏يَسْعَى وَيُقَصِّرَ ‏ ‏أَوْ يَحْلِقَ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ يُحِلَّ ‏
en: Hushaym narrated to us, Yazid ibn Abi Ziyad informed us, from Mujahid, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: The Prophet ﷺ entered ihram for Hajj. When he arrived, he performed tawaf of the House and (sa'i) between al-Safa and al-Marwah, but he did not shorten his hair and did not exit ihram because of the sacrificial animal. He commanded those who had not brought a sacrificial animal to perform tawaf and sa'i, then shorten or shave their hair and exit ihram.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هُشَيْمٌ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏جَابِرٌ الْجُعْفِيُّ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَرَّ بِقِدْرٍ فَأَخَذَ مِنْهَا ‏ ‏عَرْقًا ‏ ‏وَكَتِفًا فَأَكَلَهُ ثُمَّ صَلَّى وَلَمْ يَتَوَضَّأْ ‏
en: Hushaym narrated to us, Jabir al-Ju'fi informed us, Abu Ja'far Muhammad ibn 'Ali narrated to us, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ passed by a cooking pot and took from it a bone with meat and a shoulder blade, ate them, then prayed without performing wudu.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏هُشَيْمٌ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ أَبِي لَيْلَى ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏دَاوُدَ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏صُومُوا ‏ ‏يَوْمَ عَاشُورَاءَ ‏ ‏وَخَالِفُوا فِيهِ ‏ ‏الْيَهُودَ ‏ ‏صُومُوا قَبْلَهُ يَوْمًا أَوْ بَعْدَهُ يَوْمًا ‏
en: Hushaym said: Ibn Abi Layla informed us, from Dawud ibn 'Ali, from his father, from his grandfather Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Fast the Day of 'Ashura and differ from the Jews therein: fast a day before it or a day after it."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَابِرٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الشَّعْبِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏كَانَ ‏ ‏إِذَا ‏ ‏احْتَجَمَ ‏ ‏احْتَجَمَ ‏ ‏فِي ‏ ‏الْأَخْدَعَيْنِ ‏ ‏قَالَ فَدَعَا غُلَامًا ‏ ‏لِبَنِي بَيَاضَةَ ‏ ‏فَحَجَمَهُ ‏ ‏وَأَعْطَى ‏ ‏الْحَجَّامَ ‏ ‏أَجْرَهُ ‏ ‏مُدًّا ‏ ‏وَنِصْفًا قَالَ وَكَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَوَالِيَهُ ‏ ‏فَحَطُّوا عَنْهُ نِصْفَ ‏ ‏مُدٍّ ‏ ‏وَكَانَ عَلَيْهِ ‏ ‏مُدَّانِ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, Shu'bah narrated to us, from Jabir, from al-Sha'bi, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), that when the Messenger of Allah ﷺ would perform cupping, he would be cupped on the two jugular veins. He said: He called a slave boy belonging to Banu Bayadah, who cupped him, and he gave the cupper his wages: one and a half mudd. He said: He spoke to his masters, and they reduced half a mudd from his (daily obligation), as his obligation had been two mudd.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَابِرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏الشَّعْبِيَّ ‏ ‏يُحَدِّثُ عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عُمَرَ ‏ ‏وَابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَا ‏ ‏سَنَّ ‏ ‏رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏الصَّلَاةَ فِي السَّفَرِ رَكْعَتَيْنِ وَهِيَ تَمَامٌ ‏ ‏وَالْوَتْرُ ‏ ‏فِي السَّفَرِ سُنَّةٌ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, Shu'bah narrated to us, from Jabir, who said: I heard al-Sha'bi narrating from Ibn 'Umar and Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهم), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ established the prayer during travel as two rak'ahs, and that is complete. And witr during travel is a sunnah.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَابِرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمَّارٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏مَنْ بَنَى لِلَّهِ مَسْجِدًا وَلَوْ ‏ ‏كَمَفْحَصِ ‏ ‏قَطَاةٍ ‏ ‏لِبَيْضِهَا بَنَى اللَّهُ لَهُ بَيْتًا فِي الْجَنَّةِ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, Shu'bah narrated to us, from Jabir, from 'Ammar, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), from the Prophet ﷺ, that he said: "Whoever builds a masjid for Allah, even if it be like the nest a sand grouse scratches out for its eggs, Allah will build for him a house in Paradise."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏وَحَجَّاجٌ ‏ ‏قَالَا حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏أَبَا جَمْرَةَ الضُّبَعِيَّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏تَمَتَّعْتُ ‏ ‏فَنَهَانِي نَاسٌ عَنْ ذَلِكَ فَأَتَيْتُ ‏ ‏ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏فَسَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ ذَلِكَ فَأَمَرَنِي بِهَا قَالَ ثُمَّ انْطَلَقْتُ إِلَى الْبَيْتِ فَنِمْتُ فَأَتَانِي آتٍ فِي مَنَامِي فَقَالَ ‏ ‏عُمْرَةٌ مُتَقَبَّلَةٌ وَحَجٌّ ‏ ‏مَبْرُورٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ فَأَتَيْتُ ‏ ‏ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏فَأَخْبَرْتُهُ بِالَّذِي رَأَيْتُ فَقَالَ اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ سُنَّةُ ‏ ‏أَبِي الْقَاسِمِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَقَالَ فِي ‏ ‏الْهَدْيِ ‏ ‏جَزُورٌ ‏ ‏أَوْ بَقَرَةٌ أَوْ شَاةٌ أَوْ شِرْكٌ فِي دَمٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏عَبْد اللَّهِ ‏ ‏مَا أَسْنَدَ ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي جَمْرَةَ ‏ ‏إِلَّا وَاحِدًا ‏ ‏وَأَبُو جَمْرَةَ ‏ ‏أَوْثَقُ مِنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي حَمْزَةَ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far and Hajjaj narrated to us, both said: Shu'bah narrated to us, who said: I heard Abu Jamrah al-Duba'i, who said: I performed tamattu' (combined 'Umrah and Hajj), and some people forbade me from that. So I went to Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما) and asked him about it, and he commanded me to do it. He said: Then I went to the House (Ka'bah) and slept, and someone came to me in my dream and said: "An accepted 'Umrah and a blessed Hajj." He said: I went to Ibn 'Abbas and told him what I saw, and he said: "Allahu Akbar! Allahu Akbar! The Sunnah of Abu al-Qasim ﷺ!" And regarding the sacrificial animal, he said: "A camel, or a cow, or a sheep, or a share in a blood sacrifice." 'Abdullah said: Shu'bah only attributed one hadith from Abu Jamrah as marfu', and Abu Jamrah is more reliable than Abu Hamzah.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي السَّفَرِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَعِيدِ بْنِ شُفَيٍّ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏جَعَلَ النَّاسُ يَسْأَلُونَهُ عَنْ الصَّلَاةِ فِي السَّفَرِ فَقَالَ كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِذَا خَرَجَ مِنْ أَهْلِهِ لَمْ يُصَلِّ إِلَّا رَكْعَتَيْنِ حَتَّى يَرْجِعَ إِلَى أَهْلِهِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَسْوَدُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏إِسْرَائِيلُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَعِيدِ بْنِ شُفَيٍّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كُنْتُ عِنْدَ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏فَذَكَرَ الْحَدِيثَ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, Shu'bah narrated to us, from Abu Ishaq, from Abu al-Safar, from Sa'id ibn Shufayy, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: People kept asking him about prayer during travel. He said: When the Messenger of Allah ﷺ departed from his family, he would only pray two rak'ahs until he returned to his family. Aswad narrated to us, Isra'il narrated to us, from Abu Ishaq, from Sa'id ibn Shufayy, who said: I was with Ibn 'Abbas, and he mentioned the hadith.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سَعِيدٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏قَتَادَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏نَهَى عَنْ ‏ ‏الْمُجَثَّمَةِ ‏ ‏وَالْجَلَّالَةِ ‏ ‏وَأَنْ يُشْرَبَ مِنْ ‏ ‏فِي ‏ ‏السِّقَاءِ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, Sa'id narrated to us, from Qatadah, from 'Ikrimah, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ prohibited the mujath-thamah (animal tied and used as a target for killing), the jallalah (animal that feeds on filth), and drinking directly from the mouth of a waterskin.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سَعِيدٌ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏النَّضْرِ بْنِ أَنَسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كُنْتُ عِنْدَ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ يُفْتِي النَّاسَ لَا يُسْنِدُ إِلَى نَبِيِّ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏شَيْئًا مِنْ فُتْيَاهُ حَتَّى جَاءَهُ رَجُلٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ ‏ ‏الْعِرَاقِ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ إِنِّي رَجُلٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ ‏ ‏الْعِرَاقِ ‏ ‏وَإِنِّي أُصَوِّرُ هَذِهِ ‏ ‏التَّصَاوِيرَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ لَهُ ‏ ‏ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏ادْنُهْ ‏ ‏إِمَّا مَرَّتَيْنِ أَوْ ثَلَاثًا ‏ ‏فَدَنَا فَقَالَ ‏ ‏ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏مَنْ صَوَّرَ صُورَةً فِي الدُّنْيَا يُكَلَّفُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ أَنْ يَنْفُخَ فِيهِ الرُّوحَ وَلَيْسَ بِنَافِخٍ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, Sa'id narrated to us, from al-Nadr ibn Anas, who said: I was with Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما) while he was issuing fatwas to the people without attributing any of his fatwas to the Prophet of Allah ﷺ, until a man from Iraq came and said: "I am a man from Iraq, and I make these images (taswir)." Ibn 'Abbas said to him: "Come closer" — either two or three times — so he came closer. Then Ibn 'Abbas said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "Whoever makes an image in this world will be tasked on the Day of Judgment to blow the soul into it, and he will never be able to do so."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مَالِكٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْفَضْلِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏نَافِعِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏الْأَيِّمُ ‏ ‏أَحَقُّ بِنَفْسِهَا مِنْ ‏ ‏وَلِيِّهَا ‏ ‏وَالْبِكْرُ ‏ ‏تُسْتَأْذَنُ فِي نَفْسِهَا وَإِذْنُهَا صُمَاتُهَا ‏
en: 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Mahdi narrated to us, from Malik, from 'Abdullah ibn al-Fadl, from Nafi' ibn Jubayr, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "The previously-married woman (ayyim) has more right over herself than her guardian, and the virgin is to be asked for her consent regarding herself, and her consent is her silence."

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَرَأْتُ عَلَى ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مَالِكٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مَخْرَمَةَ بْنِ سُلَيْمَانَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏كُرَيْبٍ ‏ ‏مَوْلَى ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَهُ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ بَاتَ عِنْدَ ‏ ‏مَيْمُونَةَ زَوْجِ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَهِيَ خَالَتُهُ قَالَ فَاضْطَجَعْتُ فِي عَرْضِ الْوِسَادَةِ وَاضْطَجَعَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَأَهْلُهُ فِي طُولِهَا فَنَامَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏حَتَّى إِذَا انْتَصَفَ اللَّيْلُ ‏ ‏أَوْ قَبْلَهُ بِقَلِيلٍ أَوْ بَعْدَهُ بِقَلِيلٍ ‏ ‏اسْتَيْقَظَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَجَلَسَ يَمْسَحُ النَّوْمَ عَنْ وَجْهِهِ بِيَدِهِ ثُمَّ ‏ ‏قَرَأَ الْعَشْرَ الْآيَاتِ خَوَاتِيمَ سُورَةِ ‏ ‏آلِ ‏ ‏عِمْرَانَ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ قَامَ إِلَى ‏ ‏شَنٍّ ‏ ‏مُعَلَّقَةٍ فَتَوَضَّأَ مِنْهَا فَأَحْسَنَ وُضُوءَهُ ثُمَّ قَامَ ‏ ‏يُصَلِّي قَالَ ‏ ‏ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏فَقُمْتُ فَصَنَعْتُ مِثْلَ الَّذِي صَنَعَ ثُمَّ ذَهَبْتُ فَقُمْتُ إِلَى جَنْبِهِ فَوَضَعَ يَدَهُ عَلَى رَأْسِي وَأَخَذَ أُذُنِي الْيُمْنَى فَفَتَلَهَا فَصَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ ثُمَّ رَكْعَتَيْنِ ثُمَّ رَكْعَتَيْنِ ثُمَّ رَكْعَتَيْنِ ثُمَّ رَكْعَتَيْنِ ثُمَّ رَكْعَتَيْنِ ثُمَّ ‏ ‏أَوْتَرَ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ اضْطَجَعَ حَتَّى أَتَاهُ الْمُؤَذِّنُ فَقَامَ فَصَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ خَفِيفَتَيْنِ ثُمَّ خَرَجَ فَصَلَّى الصُّبْحَ ‏
en: He said: I recited to 'Abd al-Rahman, from Malik, from Makhramah ibn Sulayman, from Kurayb, the freed slave of Ibn 'Abbas, that 'Abdullah ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما) informed him that he spent the night at the house of Maymunah, the wife of the Prophet ﷺ, who was his maternal aunt. He said: I lay down across the width of the pillow, while the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and his wife lay along its length. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ slept until midnight — or slightly before or slightly after it — then he woke up and sat wiping the sleep from his face with his hand. Then he recited the last ten verses of Surah Al 'Imran. Then he stood up to a hanging waterskin and performed wudu from it, perfecting his wudu. Then he stood to pray. Ibn 'Abbas said: I got up and did as he had done, then went and stood beside him. He placed his hand on my head and took my right ear, twisting it gently. Then he prayed two rak'ahs, then two, then two, then two, then two, then two, then performed witr. Then he lay down until the mu'adhdhin came to him. He stood up and prayed two light rak'ahs, then went out and prayed the Fajr prayer.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمَّارِ بْنِ أَبِي عَمَّارٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏رَأَيْتُ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي الْمَنَامِ بِنِصْفِ النَّهَارِ ‏ ‏أَشْعَثَ ‏ ‏أَغْبَرَ ‏ ‏مَعَهُ ‏ ‏قَارُورَةٌ ‏ ‏فِيهَا دَمٌ يَلْتَقِطُهُ ‏ ‏أَوْ يَتَتَبَّعُ فِيهَا شَيْئًا ‏ ‏قَالَ قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ مَا هَذَا قَالَ دَمُ ‏ ‏الْحُسَيْنِ ‏ ‏وَأَصْحَابِهِ لَمْ أَزَلْ أَتَتَبَّعُهُ مُنْذُ الْيَوْمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏عَمَّارٌ ‏ ‏فَحَفِظْنَا ذَلِكَ الْيَوْمَ فَوَجَدْنَاهُ قُتِلَ ذَلِكَ الْيَوْمَ ‏
en: 'Abd al-Rahman narrated to us, Hammad ibn Salamah narrated to us, from 'Ammar ibn Abi 'Ammar, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: I saw the Prophet ﷺ in a dream at midday, disheveled and dusty, with a bottle in which was blood that he was collecting — or following something in it. I said: "O Messenger of Allah, what is this?" He said: "The blood of al-Husayn and his companions. I have been following it since today." 'Ammar said: We noted that day, and we found that he (al-Husayn) was killed on that very day.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُفْيَانُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ بْنِ كُهَيْلٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عِمْرَانَ أَبِي الْحَكَمِ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَتْ ‏ ‏قُرَيْشٌ ‏ ‏لِلنَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏ادْعُ لَنَا رَبَّكَ أَنْ يَجْعَلَ لَنَا ‏ ‏الصَّفَا ‏ ‏ذَهَبًا وَنُؤْمِنُ بِكَ قَالَ وَتَفْعَلُونَ قَالُوا نَعَمْ قَالَ فَدَعَا فَأَتَاهُ ‏ ‏جِبْرِيلُ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ يَقْرَأُ عَلَيْكَ السَّلَامَ وَيَقُولُ ‏ ‏إِنْ شِئْتَ أَصْبَحَ لَهُمْ ‏ ‏الصَّفَا ‏ ‏ذَهَبًا فَمَنْ كَفَرَ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ مِنْهُمْ عَذَّبْتُهُ عَذَابًا لَا أُعَذِّبُهُ أَحَدًا مِنْ الْعَالَمِينَ وَإِنْ شِئْتَ فَتَحْتُ لَهُمْ بَابَ التَّوْبَةِ وَالرَّحْمَةِ قَالَ بَلْ بَابُ التَّوْبَةِ وَالرَّحْمَةِ ‏
en: 'Abd al-Rahman narrated to us, Sufyan narrated to us, from Salamah ibn Kuhayl, from 'Imran Abu al-Hakam, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: The Quraysh said to the Prophet ﷺ: "Call upon your Lord to turn al-Safa into gold for us, and we will believe in you." He said: "Will you really do that?" They said: "Yes." So he supplicated, and Jibril (عليه السلام) came to him and said: "Your Lord عز وجل sends you His peace and says: If you wish, al-Safa will become gold for them in the morning, but whoever disbelieves after that among them, I will punish him with a punishment I have not inflicted on anyone in all the worlds. And if you wish, I will open for them the door of repentance and mercy." He said: "Rather, the door of repentance and mercy."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏قَتَادَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏أَبَا الْعَالِيَةِ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏ابْنُ عَمِّ نَبِيِّكُمْ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَعْنِي ‏ ‏ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لَا يَنْبَغِي لِأَحَدٍ أَنْ يَقُولَ أَنَا خَيْرٌ مِنْ ‏ ‏يُونُسَ بْنِ مَتَّى ‏ ‏وَنَسَبَهُ إِلَى أَبِيهِ ‏
en: 'Abd al-Rahman narrated to us, Shu'bah narrated to us, from Qatadah, who said: I heard Abu al-'Aliyah saying: The cousin of your Prophet ﷺ — meaning Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما) — narrated to me. He said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "It is not befitting for anyone to say: 'I am better than Yunus ibn Matta,'" and he attributed him to his father.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَرَأْتُ عَلَى ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مَالِكٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي الزُّبَيْرِ الْمَكِّيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏طَاوُسٍ الْيَمَانِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏كَانَ يُعَلِّمُهُمْ الدُّعَاءَ كَمَا يُعَلِّمُهُمْ السُّورَةَ مِنْ الْقُرْآنِ يَقُولُ قُولُوا ‏ ‏اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ عَذَابِ جَهَنَّمَ وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ عَذَابِ الْقَبْرِ وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ فِتْنَةِ ‏ ‏الْمَسِيحِ الدَّجَّالِ ‏ ‏وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ فِتْنَةِ الْمَحْيَا وَالْمَمَاتِ ‏
en: He said: I recited to 'Abd al-Rahman, from Malik, from Abu al-Zubayr al-Makki, from Tawus al-Yamani, from 'Abdullah ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to teach them this supplication just as he would teach them a surah from the Quran. He would say: "Say: 'O Allah, I seek refuge in You from the punishment of Hellfire, and I seek refuge in You from the punishment of the grave, and I seek refuge in You from the trial of al-Masih al-Dajjal, and I seek refuge in You from the trials of life and death (Allahumma inni a'udhu bika min 'adhabi Jahannam, wa a'udhu bika min 'adhabi al-qabr, wa a'udhu bika min fitnati al-Masih al-Dajjal, wa a'udhu bika min fitnati al-mahya wa al-mamat).'"

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ يَزِيدَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏دَاوُدَ يَعْنِي ابْنَ أَبِي الْفُرَاتِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏إِبْرَاهِيمَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏صَلَّى نَبِيُّ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏بِالنَّاسِ يَوْمَ فِطْرٍ رَكْعَتَيْنِ بِغَيْرِ أَذَانٍ وَلَا إِقَامَةٍ ثُمَّ خَطَبَ بَعْدَ الصَّلَاةِ ثُمَّ أَخَذَ بِيَدِ ‏ ‏بِلَالٍ ‏ ‏فَانْطَلَقَ إِلَى النِّسَاءِ فَخَطَبَهُنَّ ثُمَّ أَمَرَ ‏ ‏بِلَالًا ‏ ‏بَعْدَمَا ‏ ‏قَفَّى ‏ ‏مِنْ عِنْدِهِنَّ ‏ ‏أَنْ يَأْتِيَهُنَّ فَيَأْمُرَهُنَّ أَنْ يَتَصَدَّقْنَ ‏
en: 'Abdullah ibn Yazid narrated to us, from Dawud, meaning Ibn Abi al-Furat, from Ibrahim, from 'Ata', from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: The Prophet of Allah ﷺ prayed the Eid al-Fitr prayer with the people: two rak'ahs without adhan or iqamah. Then he gave the sermon after the prayer. Then he took Bilal by the hand and went to the women and addressed them. Then he ordered Bilal, after he had turned away from them, to go to them and command them to give charity.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ حَنْبَلٍ ‏ ‏مِنْ كِتَابِهِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ الْأُمَوِيُّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏الْأَعْمَشُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا عَنْ ‏ ‏طَارِقٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ قَالَ ‏ ‏ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّكَ أَذَقْتَ أَوَائِلَ ‏ ‏قُرَيْشٍ ‏ ‏نَكَالًا ‏ ‏فَأَذِقْ آخِرَهُمْ ‏ ‏نَوَالًا ‏
en: My father Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Hanbal narrated to me from his book, Yahya ibn Sa'id al-Umawi narrated to us, al-A'mash said: he narrated to us from Tariq, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, who said: Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما) said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "O Allah, You made the early ones of Quraysh taste punishment, so make the later ones taste bounty."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ رَبِيعَةَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ جُرَيْجٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏طَاوُسٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏شَهِدْتُ مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏الْعِيدَ ‏ ‏وَأَبِي بَكْرٍ ‏ ‏وَعُمَرَ ‏ ‏وَعُثْمَانَ ‏ ‏فَكُلُّهُمْ ‏ ‏صَلَّى قَبْلَ الْخُطْبَةِ بِغَيْرِ أَذَانٍ وَلَا إِقَامَةٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ رَبِيعَةَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ جُرَيْجٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَابِرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏بِمِثْلِ ذَلِكَ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Rabi'ah narrated to us, Ibn Jurayj narrated to us, from al-Hasan ibn Muslim, from Tawus, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: I attended the Eid with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, Abu Bakr, 'Umar, and 'Uthman (رضي الله عنهم), and all of them prayed before the sermon without adhan or iqamah. Muhammad ibn Rabi'ah narrated to us, Ibn Jurayj narrated to us, from 'Ata', from Jabir, from the Prophet ﷺ, a similar report.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُؤَمَّلٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُفْيَانُ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏طَاوُسٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏صَلَّى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏الْعِيدَ ثُمَّ خَطَبَ وَصَلَّى ‏ ‏أَبُو بَكْرٍ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ خَطَبَ ‏ ‏وَعُمَرُ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ خَطَبَ ‏ ‏وَعُثْمَانُ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ خَطَبَ بِغَيْرِ أَذَانٍ وَلَا إِقَامَةٍ ‏
en: Mu'ammal narrated to us, Sufyan narrated to us, from Ibn Jurayj, from al-Hasan ibn Muslim, from Tawus, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ prayed the Eid prayer then gave the sermon, and Abu Bakr prayed then gave the sermon, and 'Umar prayed then gave the sermon, and 'Uthman prayed then gave the sermon — without adhan or iqamah.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْقَاسِمُ بْنُ مَالِكٍ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏حَنْظَلَةَ السَّدُوسِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏شَهْرِ بْنِ حَوْشَبٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏صَلَّى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏الْعِيدَ رَكْعَتَيْنِ لَا يَقْرَأُ فِيهِمَا إِلَّا ‏ ‏بِأُمِّ الْكِتَابِ ‏ ‏لَمْ يَزِدْ عَلَيْهَا شَيْئًا ‏
en: Al-Qasim ibn Malik Abu Ja'far narrated to us, from Hanzalah al-Sadusi, from Shahr ibn Hawshab, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ prayed the Eid prayer as two rak'ahs, in which he recited nothing except Umm al-Kitab (al-Fatihah), adding nothing more to it.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ بْنُ أَبِي حَكِيمٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْحَكَمُ يَعْنِي ابْنَ أَبَانَ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ قَالَ ‏ ‏ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏رُكِزَتْ ‏ ‏الْعَنَزَةُ ‏ ‏بَيْنَ يَدَيْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏بِعَرَفَاتٍ ‏ ‏فَصَلَّى إِلَيْهَا وَالْحِمَارُ يَمُرُّ مِنْ وَرَاءِ ‏ ‏الْعَنَزَةِ ‏
en: Yazid ibn Abi Hakim narrated to us, al-Hakam, meaning Ibn Aban, narrated to us, who said: I heard 'Ikrimah saying: Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما) said: A short spear ('anazah) was planted before the Prophet ﷺ at 'Arafat, and he prayed facing it while a donkey passed behind the spear.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الْقُدُّوسِ بْنُ بَكْرِ بْنِ خُنَيْسٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْحَجَّاجُ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْحَكَمِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مِقْسَمٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَاصَرَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَهْلَ ‏ ‏الطَّائِفِ ‏ ‏فَخَرَجَ إِلَيْهِ عَبْدَانِ فَأَعْتَقَهُمَا أَحَدُهُمَا ‏ ‏أَبُو بَكْرَةَ ‏ ‏وَكَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يُعْتِقُ الْعَبِيدَ إِذَا خَرَجُوا إِلَيْهِ ‏
en: 'Abd al-Quddus ibn Bakr ibn Khunays narrated to us, al-Hajjaj narrated to us, from al-Hakam, from Miqsam, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ besieged the people of al-Ta'if, and two slaves came out to him, so he freed them. One of them was Abu Bakrah (رضي الله عنه). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to free slaves when they came out to him.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْقَاسِمُ بْنُ مَالِكٍ الْمُزَنِيُّ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَيُّوبَ بْنِ عَائِذٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏بُكَيْرِ بْنِ الْأَخْنَسِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُجَاهِدٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ فَرَضَ الصَّلَاةَ عَلَى لِسَانِ نَبِيِّكُمْ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي الْحَضَرِ أَرْبَعًا وَفِي السَّفَرِ رَكْعَتَيْنِ وَفِي الْخَوْفِ رَكْعَةً ‏
en: Al-Qasim ibn Malik al-Muzani Abu Ja'far narrated to us, from Ayyub ibn 'A'idh, from Bukayr ibn al-Akhnas, from Mujahid, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: Indeed Allah عز وجل prescribed the prayer upon the tongue of your Prophet ﷺ: in residence four rak'ahs, in travel two rak'ahs, and in fear one rak'ah.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَمَّارُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏ابْنُ أُخْتِ ‏ ‏سُفْيَانَ الثَّوْرِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مَنْصُورٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَالِمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏كُرَيْبٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَيَعْجِزُ أَحَدُكُمْ إِذَا ‏ ‏أَتَى ‏ ‏أَهْلَهُ أَنْ يَقُولَ بِسْمِ اللَّهِ اللَّهُمَّ جَنِّبْنِي الشَّيْطَانَ وَجَنِّبْ الشَّيْطَانَ مَا رَزَقْتَنِي فَإِنْ اللَّهُ قَضَى بَيْنَهُمَا فِي ذَلِكَ وَلَدًا لَمْ يَضُرَّهُ الشَّيْطَانُ أَبَدًا ‏
en: 'Ammar ibn Muhammad, the nephew of Sufyan al-Thawri, narrated to us, from Mansur, from Salim, from Kurayb, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Is one of you incapable, when he comes to his wife, of saying: 'Bismillah. O Allah, keep the Shaytan away from me and keep the Shaytan away from what You provide us.' For if Allah decrees a child between them from that union, the Shaytan will never harm that child."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَاصِمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ لِي ‏ ‏ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏يَا ‏ ‏سَعِيدُ ‏ ‏أَلَكَ امْرَأَةٌ قَالَ قُلْتُ لَا قَالَ فَإِذَا رَجَعْتَ فَتَزَوَّجْ قَالَ فَعُدْتُ إِلَيْهِ فَقَالَ يَا ‏ ‏سَعِيدُ ‏ ‏أَتَزَوَّجْتَ قَالَ قُلْتُ لَا قَالَ ‏ ‏تَزَوَّجْ فَإِنَّ خَيْرَ هَذِهِ الْأُمَّةِ أَكْثَرُهُمْ نِسَاءً ‏
en: 'Ali ibn 'Asim narrated to us, from 'Ata', from Sa'id, who said: Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما) said to me: "O Sa'id, do you have a wife?" I said: "No." He said: "When you go back, get married." He said: I returned to him, and he said: "O Sa'id, did you get married?" I said: "No." He said: "Get married, for the best of this Ummah had the most wives."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَاصِمٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو عَلِيٍّ الرَّحَبِيُّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةَ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏اغْتَسَلَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مِنْ جَنَابَةٍ فَلَمَّا خَرَجَ رَأَى لُمْعَةً عَلَى ‏ ‏مَنْكِبِهِ ‏ ‏الْأَيْسَرِ لَمْ يُصِبْهَا الْمَاءُ فَأَخَذَ مِنْ شَعَرِهِ فَبَلَّهَا ثُمَّ مَضَى إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ ‏
en: 'Ali ibn 'Asim narrated to us, Abu 'Ali al-Rahabi narrated to us, from 'Ikrimah, Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما) informed us, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed ghusl from janabah. When he came out, he noticed a spot on his left shoulder that the water had not reached. He took some of his hair (wet from the ghusl) and moistened it, then went to the prayer.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو الْيَمَانِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏ثَعْلَبَةَ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ الْخَثْعَمِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي كَعْبٍ ‏ ‏مَوْلَى ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قِيلَ لَهُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ لَقَدْ أَبْطَأَ عَنْكَ ‏ ‏جِبْرِيلُ ‏ ‏عَلَيْهِ السَّلَام ‏ ‏فَقَالَ ‏ ‏وَلِمَ لَا يُبْطِئُ عَنِّي وَأَنْتُمْ حَوْلِي لَا ‏ ‏تَسْتَنُّونَ ‏ ‏وَلَا تُقَلِّمُونَ أَظْفَارَكُمْ وَلَا تَقُصُّونَ شَوَارِبَكُمْ وَلَا تُنَقُّونَ ‏ ‏رَوَاجِبَكُمْ ‏
en: Abu al-Yaman narrated to us, Isma'il ibn 'Ayyash narrated to us, from Tha'labah ibn Muslim al-Khath'ami, from Abu Ka'b, the freed slave of Ibn 'Abbas, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), from the Prophet ﷺ, that it was said to him: "O Messenger of Allah, Jibril (عليه السلام) has been slow in coming to you." He said: "Why would he not be slow when you are around me and you do not use the siwak, you do not trim your nails, you do not clip your mustaches, and you do not clean your teeth?"

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هَاشِمُ بْنُ الْقَاسِمِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي خَالِدٍ يَزِيدَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْمِنْهَالِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏مَنْ أَتَى مَرِيضًا لَمْ يَحْضُرْ أَجَلُهُ فَقَالَ سَبْعَ مَرَّاتٍ أَسْأَلُ اللَّهَ الْعَظِيمَ رَبَّ الْعَرْشِ الْكَرِيمِ أَنْ يَشْفِيَهُ إِلَّا عُوفِيَ ‏
en: Hashim ibn al-Qasim narrated to us, Shu'bah narrated to us, from Abu Khalid Yazid, from al-Minhal ibn 'Amr, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: "Whoever comes to a sick person whose death has not yet arrived, and says seven times: 'I ask Allah the Almighty, Lord of the Noble Throne, to cure him (As'alullaha al-'Azim, Rabba al-'Arsh al-Karim, an yashfiyahu),' he will be cured."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هَاشِمٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَاصِمٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الشَّعْبِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏مَرَّ بِي النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَرِيبًا مِنْ ‏ ‏زَمْزَمَ ‏ ‏فَدَعَا بِمَاءٍ وَاسْتَسْقَى فَأَتَيْتُهُ بِدَلْوٍ مِنْ ‏ ‏زَمْزَمَ ‏ ‏فَشَرِبَ وَهُوَ قَائِمٌ ‏
en: Hashim narrated to us, Shu'bah narrated to us, from 'Asim, from al-Sha'bi, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: The Prophet ﷺ passed by me near Zamzam and called for water and asked for a drink. I brought him a bucket from Zamzam, and he drank while standing.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُلَيْمَانُ بْنُ دَاوُدَ الْهَاشِمِيُّ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏صَالِحُ بْنُ كَيْسَانَ ‏ ‏وَابْنُ أَخِي ابْنِ شِهَابٍ ‏ ‏كِلَاهُمَا ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ شِهَابٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏وَيَعْقُوبُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏صَالِحٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏ابْنُ شِهَابٍ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنِي ‏ ‏عُبَيْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏بَعَثَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ حُذَافَةَ ‏ ‏بِكِتَابِهِ إِلَى ‏ ‏كِسْرَى ‏ ‏قَالَ فَدَفَعَهُ إِلَى عَظِيمِ ‏ ‏الْبَحْرَيْنِ ‏ ‏يَدْفَعُهُ عَظِيمُ ‏ ‏الْبَحْرَيْنِ ‏ ‏إِلَى ‏ ‏كِسْرَى ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏يَعْقُوبُ ‏ ‏فَدَفَعَهُ عَظِيمُ ‏ ‏الْبَحْرَيْنِ ‏ ‏إِلَى ‏ ‏كِسْرَى ‏ ‏فَلَمَّا قَرَأَهُ مَزَّقَهُ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏ابْنُ شِهَابٍ ‏ ‏فَحَسِبْتُ ‏ ‏ابْنَ الْمُسَيَّبِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏فَدَعَا عَلَيْهِمْ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏بِأَنْ يُمَزَّقُوا كُلَّ مُمَزَّقٍ ‏
en: Sulayman ibn Dawud al-Hashimi narrated to us, Ibrahim ibn Sa'd narrated to us, who said: Salih ibn Kaysan and the nephew of Ibn Shihab both narrated to me, from Ibn Shihab, from 'Ubaydullah ibn 'Abdullah, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما). And Ya'qub said: My father narrated to me, from Salih. Ibn Shihab said: 'Ubaydullah ibn 'Abdullah informed me that Ibn 'Abbas informed him, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent 'Abdullah ibn Hudhafah with his letter to Kisra (the Persian emperor). He said: He handed it to the governor of Bahrain, and the governor of Bahrain handed it to Kisra. Ya'qub said: The governor of Bahrain handed it to Kisra, and when he read it, he tore it up. Ibn Shihab said: I think Ibn al-Musayyab said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ supplicated against them, that they be torn apart completely.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هَاشِمٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْحَكَمِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مِقْسَمٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏صَامَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَوْمَ فَتْحِ ‏ ‏مَكَّةَ ‏ ‏حَتَّى أَتَى ‏ ‏قُدَيْدًا ‏ ‏فَأُتِيَ ‏ ‏بِقَدَحٍ ‏ ‏مِنْ لَبَنٍ فَأَفْطَرَ ‏ ‏وَأَمَرَ النَّاسَ أَنْ يُفْطِرُوا ‏
en: Hashim narrated to us, Shu'bah narrated to us, from al-Hakam, from Miqsam, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ was fasting on the day of the conquest of Makkah until he reached Qudayd, where he was brought a bowl of milk. He broke his fast and ordered the people to break their fast.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هَاشِمٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْحَكَمِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مِقْسَمٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏احْتَجَمَ ‏ ‏بِالْقَاحَةِ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ صَائِمٌ ‏
en: Hashim narrated to us, Shu'bah narrated to us, from al-Hakam, from Miqsam, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was cupped at al-Qahah while he was fasting.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حُجَيْنُ بْنُ الْمُثَنَّى ‏ ‏وَيُونُسُ يَعْنِي ابْنَ مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏قَالَا حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الْعَزِيزِ يَعْنِي ابْنَ أَبِي سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ عُقْبَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏كُرَيْبٍ ‏ ‏مَوْلَى ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏مَرَّ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏عَلَى امْرَأَةٍ وَمَعَهَا صَبِيٌّ لَهَا فِي ‏ ‏مِحَفَّةٍ ‏ ‏فَأَخَذَتْ ‏ ‏بِضَبْعِهِ ‏ ‏فَقَالَتْ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ أَلِهَذَا حَجٌّ قَالَ نَعَمْ وَلَكِ أَجْرٌ ‏
en: Hujayn ibn al-Muthanna and Yunus, meaning Ibn Muhammad, narrated to us, both said: 'Abd al-'Aziz, meaning Ibn Abi Salamah, narrated to us, from Ibrahim ibn 'Uqbah, from Kurayb, the freed slave of 'Abdullah ibn 'Abbas, from 'Abdullah ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: The Prophet ﷺ passed by a woman who had her child with her in a litter. She took hold of his arm and said: "O Messenger of Allah, is there Hajj for this one?" He said: "Yes, and you will have a reward."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يُونُسُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادٌ يَعْنِي ابْنَ زَيْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَيُّوبَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سِيرِينَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏إِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏تَعَرَّقَ ‏ ‏كَتِفًا ثُمَّ قَامَ فَصَلَّى وَلَمْ يَتَوَضَّأْ ‏
en: Yunus narrated to us, Hammad, meaning Ibn Zayd, narrated to us, from Ayyub, from Muhammad ibn Sirin, that Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما) narrated to him, saying: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ gnawed meat from a shoulder bone, then stood and prayed without performing wudu.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يُونُسُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادٌ يَعْنِي ابْنَ زَيْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي التَّيَّاحِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُوسَى بْنِ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏خَرَجْتُ أَنَا ‏ ‏وَسِنَانُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏وَمَعَنَا ‏ ‏بَدَنَتَانِ ‏ ‏فَأَزْحَفَتَا ‏ ‏عَلَيْنَا فِي الطَّرِيقِ فَقَالَ لِي ‏ ‏سِنَانٌِ ‏ ‏هَلْ لَكَ فِي ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏فَأَتَيْنَاهُ فَسَأَلَهُ ‏ ‏سِنَانٌ ‏ ‏فَذَكَرَ الْحَدِيثَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏وَقَالَ ‏ ‏ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏سَأَلَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏الْجُهَنِيُّ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ إِنَّ أَبِي شَيْخٌ كَبِيرٌ لَمْ يَحُجَّ قَالَ ‏ ‏حُجَّ عَنْ أَبِيكَ ‏
en: Yunus ibn Muhammad narrated to us, Hammad, meaning Ibn Zayd, narrated to us, from Abu al-Tayyah, from Musa ibn Salamah, who said: I went out along with Sinan ibn Salamah, and with us were two sacrificial camels. They became exhausted on the road. Sinan said to me: "Shall we go to Ibn 'Abbas?" So we went to him and Sinan asked him, and he mentioned the hadith. He said: Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما) said: A man from Juhaynah asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and said: "O Messenger of Allah, my father is an old man and has not performed Hajj." He said: "Perform Hajj on behalf of your father."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يُونُسُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏فُلَيْحٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏زَيْدِ بْنِ أَسْلَمَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ وَعْلَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏سَأَلْتُ ‏ ‏ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏فَقُلْتُ إِنَّا بِأَرْضٍ لَنَا بِهَا الْكُرُومُ وَإِنَّ أَكْثَرَ غَلَّاتِهَا الْخَمْرُ فَقَالَ قَدِمَ ‏ ‏رَجُلٌ ‏ ‏مِنْ ‏ ‏دَوْسٍ ‏ ‏عَلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏بِرَاوِيَةِ ‏ ‏خَمْرٍ أَهْدَاهَا لَهُ فَقَالَ لَهُ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏هَلْ عَلِمْتَ أَنَّ اللَّهَ حَرَّمَهَا بَعْدَكَ فَأَقْبَلَ صَاحِبُ ‏ ‏الرَّاوِيَةِ ‏ ‏عَلَى إِنْسَانٍ مَعَهُ فَأَمَرَهُ فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏بِمَاذَا أَمَرْتَهُ قَالَ بِبَيْعِهَا قَالَ هَلْ عَلِمْتَ أَنَّ الَّذِي حَرَّمَ شُرْبَهَا حَرَّمَ بَيْعَهَا وَأَكْلَ ثَمَنِهَا قَالَ فَأَمَرَ ‏ ‏بِالْمَزَادَةِ ‏ ‏فَأُهْرِيقَتْ ‏
en: Yunus narrated to us, Fulayh narrated to us, from Zayd ibn Aslam, from 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Wa'lah, who said: I asked Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما) and said: "We live in a land that has vineyards, and most of its produce is wine." He said: A man from Daws came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ with a large waterskin of wine as a gift for him. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to him: "Do you know that Allah has prohibited it after you (left)?" The owner of the waterskin turned to a man with him and gave him an order. The Prophet ﷺ said: "What did you order him to do?" He said: "To sell it." He said: "Do you know that the One who prohibited drinking it also prohibited selling it and consuming its price?" He said: So he ordered the waterskin to be poured out, and it was poured out.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يُونُسُ ‏ ‏وَحَسَنُ بْنُ مُوسَى ‏ ‏الْمَعْنَى ‏ ‏قَالَا حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادٌ يَعْنِي ابْنَ زَيْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَيُّوبَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي قِلَابَةَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏لَا أَعْلَمُهُ إِلَّا قَدْ رَفَعَهُ قَالَ كَانَ ‏ ‏إِذَا نَزَلَ مَنْزِلًا فَأَعْجَبَهُ الْمَنْزِلُ أَخَّرَ الظُّهْرَ حَتَّى يَجْمَعَ بَيْنَ الظُّهْرِ وَالْعَصْرِ وَإِذَا سَارَ وَلَمْ يَتَهَيَّأْ لَهُ الْمَنْزِلُ أَخَّرَ الظُّهْرَ حَتَّى يَأْتِيَ الْمَنْزِلَ فَيَجْمَعَ بَيْنَ الظُّهْرِ وَالْعَصْرِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَسَنٌ ‏ ‏كَانَ إِذَا سَافَرَ أَلِنَزَلَ مَنْزِلًا ‏
en: Yunus and Hasan ibn Musa narrated to us with the same meaning, both said: Hammad, meaning Ibn Zayd, narrated to us, from Ayyub, from Abu Qilabah, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: I do not know except that he raised it (to the Prophet ﷺ). He said: When he stopped at a place and liked the stopping point, he would delay Dhuhr until he could combine Dhuhr and 'Asr. And when he was traveling and the stopping point was not ready for him, he would delay Dhuhr until he reached the stopping point and combine Dhuhr and 'Asr. Hasan said: When he would travel and stop at a place.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَيُّوبُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو عَوَانَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي بِشْرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مَيْمُونِ بْنِ مِهْرَانَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏نَهَى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏عَنْ كُلِّ ذِي ‏ ‏نَابٍ مِنْ السِّبَاعِ وَعَنْ كُلِّ ذِي مِخْلَبٍ مِنْ الطَّيْرِ ‏
en: Ayyub narrated to us, Abu 'Awanah narrated to us, from Abu Bishr, from Maymun ibn Mihran, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ prohibited every predatory animal with fangs and every bird with talons.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يُونُسُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادٌ يَعْنِي ابْنَ زَيْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏كَثِيرِ بْنِ شِنْظِيرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏إِنَّمَا كَانَ بَدْءُ ‏ ‏الْإِيضَاعِ ‏ ‏مِنْ قِبَلِ أَهْلِ الْبَادِيَةِ كَانُوا يَقِفُونَ حَافَتَيْ النَّاسِ حَتَّى يُعَلِّقُوا الْعِصِيَّ ‏ ‏وَالْجِعَابَ ‏ ‏وَالْقِعَابَ ‏ ‏فَإِذَا نَفَرُوا ‏ ‏تَقَعْقَعَتْ ‏ ‏تِلْكَ فَنَفَرُوا بِالنَّاسِ قَالَ وَلَقَدْ رُئِيَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَإِنَّ ‏ ‏ذِفْرَىْ نَاقَتِهِ لَيَمَسُّ ‏ ‏حَارِكَهَا ‏ ‏وَهُوَ يَقُولُ بِيَدِهِ يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ ‏ ‏عَلَيْكُمْ ‏ ‏بِالسَّكِينَةِ ‏ ‏يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ عَلَيْكُمْ ‏ ‏بِالسَّكِينَةِ ‏
en: Yunus narrated to us, Hammad, meaning Ibn Zayd, narrated to us, from Kathir ibn Shinzir, from 'Ata', from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: The origin of the fast riding (ida') was from the desert people. They would stand on the edges of the crowd and hang their sticks, quivers, and wooden bowls. When they departed, those items would rattle and cause the people to rush. He said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ was seen with the bone behind his camel's ear touching her withers (pulling the reins to slow her), while gesturing with his hand saying: "O people, you must maintain calmness! O people, you must maintain calmness!"

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يُونُسُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏حُمَيْدٍ ‏ ‏وَأَيُّوبَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏نَامَ حَتَّى سُمِعَ لَهُ ‏ ‏غَطِيطٌ ‏ ‏فَقَامَ فَصَلَّى وَلَمْ يَتَوَضَّأْ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةُ ‏ ‏كَانَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَحْفُوظًا ‏
en: Yunus narrated to us, Hammad ibn Salamah narrated to us, from Humayd and Ayyub, from 'Ikrimah, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ slept until snoring was heard from him, then he stood and prayed without performing wudu. 'Ikrimah said: The Prophet ﷺ was protected (by Allah, so his sleep did not break his wudu).

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يُونُسُ ‏ ‏وَعَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَا حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَيُّوبَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَمَّادٌ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏أَيُّوبُ ‏ ‏وَقَيْسٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَطَاءِ بْنِ أَبِي رَبَاحٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَخَّرَ الْعِشَاءَ ذَاتَ لَيْلَةٍ حَتَّى نَامَ الْقَوْمُ ثُمَّ اسْتَيْقَظُوا ثُمَّ نَامُوا ثُمَّ اسْتَيْقَظُوا قَالَ ‏ ‏قَيْسٌ ‏ ‏فَجَاءَ ‏ ‏عُمَرُ بْنُ الْخَطَّابِ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ الصَّلَاةَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ قَالَ ‏ ‏فَخَرَجَ فَصَلَّى بِهِمْ وَلَمْ يَذْكُرْ أَنَّهُمْ تَوَضَّئُوا ‏
en: Yunus and 'Affan narrated to us, both said: Hammad ibn Salamah narrated to us, from Ayyub. 'Affan said: Hammad said: Ayyub and Qays informed us, from 'Ata' ibn Abi Rabah, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ delayed the 'Isha prayer one night until the people slept, then woke up, then slept, then woke up. Qays said: 'Umar ibn al-Khattab (رضي الله عنه) came and said: "The prayer, O Messenger of Allah!" He said: So he came out and prayed with them, and it was not mentioned that they performed wudu.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يُونُسُ ‏ ‏وَحَسَنٌ ‏ ‏قَالَا حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ دِينَارٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏كُرَيْبِ بْنِ أَبِي مُسْلِمٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏كَانَ فِي بَيْتِ ‏ ‏مَيْمُونَةَ بِنْتِ الْحَارِثِ ‏ ‏فَقَامَ ‏ ‏يُصَلِّي مِنْ اللَّيْلِ قَالَ فَقُمْتُ عَنْ يَسَارِهِ فَأَخَذَ بِيَدِي ‏ ‏فَأَقَامَنِي ‏ ‏عَنْ يَمِينِهِ ثُمَّ صَلَّى ثُمَّ نَامَ حَتَّى ‏ ‏نَفَخَ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ جَاءَهُ ‏ ‏بِلَالٌ ‏ ‏بِالْأَذَانِ فَقَامَ فَصَلَّى وَلَمْ يَتَوَضَّأْ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَسَنٌ ‏ ‏يَعْنِي فِي حَدِيثِهِ ‏ ‏كُنْتُ مَعَ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي بَيْتِ ‏ ‏مَيْمُونَةَ ‏ ‏فَلَمَّا قَضَى صَلَاتَهُ نَامَ حَتَّى ‏ ‏نَفَخَ ‏
en: Yunus and Hasan narrated to us, both said: Hammad ibn Salamah narrated to us, from 'Amr ibn Dinar, from Kurayb ibn Abi Muslim, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was in the house of Maymunah bint al-Harith. He stood to pray at night. He said: I stood on his left, and he took my hand and moved me to his right. Then he prayed, then he slept until he began to breathe deeply (with a blowing sound). Then Bilal came to him with the call to prayer, so he stood and prayed without performing wudu. Hasan said in his narration: I was with the Prophet ﷺ in the house of Maymunah. When he finished his prayer, he slept until he began to breathe deeply.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يُونُسُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شَيْبَانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏قَتَادَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي الْعَالِيَةِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ عَمِّ نَبِيِّكُمْ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ نَبِيُّ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏رَأَيْتُ لَيْلَةَ ‏ ‏أُسْرِيَ ‏ ‏بِي ‏ ‏مُوسَى بْنَ عِمْرَانَ ‏ ‏رَجُلًا ‏ ‏آدَمَ ‏ ‏طُوَالًا ‏ ‏جَعْدًا ‏ ‏كَأَنَّهُ مِنْ رِجَالِ ‏ ‏شَنُوءَةَ ‏ ‏وَرَأَيْتُ ‏ ‏عِيسَى ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ ‏ ‏عَلَيْهِمَا السَّلَام ‏ ‏مَرْبُوعَ ‏ ‏الْخَلْقِ إِلَى الْحُمْرَةِ وَالْبَيَاضِ ‏ ‏سَبْطَ ‏ ‏الرَّأْسِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حُسَيْنٌ ‏ ‏فِي تَفْسِيرِ ‏ ‏شَيْبَانَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏قَتَادَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو الْعَالِيَةِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ عَمِّ نَبِيِّكُمْ ‏ ‏ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَذَكَرَ مِثْلَهُ ‏
en: Yunus narrated to us, Shayban narrated to us, Qatadah narrated to us, from Abu al-'Aliyah, who said: The cousin of your Prophet ﷺ, Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), narrated to us. He said: The Prophet of Allah ﷺ said: "On the night I was taken on the Night Journey, I saw Musa ibn 'Imran (عليه السلام) — a dark-skinned, tall, curly-haired man, as if he were one of the men of Shanu'ah. And I saw 'Isa ibn Maryam (عليهما السلام) — of medium build, reddish-white in complexion, with straight hair." Husayn narrated to us in the tafsir of Shayban, from Qatadah, who said: Abu al-'Aliyah narrated to us, the cousin of your Prophet, Ibn 'Abbas, narrated to us. He said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said the same.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ رَبِيعَةَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبَّادُ بْنُ مَنْصُورٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَضَى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي ابْنِ ‏ ‏الْمُلَاعَنَةِ ‏ ‏أَنْ ‏ ‏لَا يُدْعَى لِأَبٍ وَمَنْ ‏ ‏رَمَاهَا ‏ ‏أَوْ ‏ ‏رَمَى ‏ ‏وَلَدَهَا فَإِنَّهُ يُجْلَدُ ‏ ‏الْحَدَّ ‏ ‏وَقَضَى أَنْ لَا ‏ ‏قُوتَ ‏ ‏لَهَا وَلَا سُكْنَى مِنْ أَجْلِ أَنَّهُمَا يَتَفَرَّقَانِ مِنْ غَيْرِ طَلَاقٍ وَلَا مُتَوَفًّى عَنْهَا ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Rabi'ah narrated to us, 'Abbad ibn Mansur narrated to us, from 'Ikrimah, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ ruled regarding the child of li'an (mutual cursing) that he is not to be attributed to any father, and whoever accuses her or accuses her child shall receive the hadd punishment. He also ruled that she has no right to maintenance or housing, because they are separating without divorce and without her being widowed.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يُونُسُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏حُمَيْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏تَزَوَّجَ ‏ ‏مَيْمُونَةَ بِنْتَ الْحَارِثِ ‏ ‏وَهُمَا مُحْرِمَانِ ‏
en: Yunus narrated to us, Hammad ibn Salamah narrated to us, from Humayd, from 'Ikrimah, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), that the Prophet ﷺ married Maymunah bint al-Harith while they were both in a state of ihram.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يُونُسُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَطَاءٍ الْعَطَّارِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏يَتَصَدَّقُ بِدِينَارٍ فَإِنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ فَنِصْفُ دِينَارٍ ‏ ‏يَعْنِي الَّذِي ‏ ‏يَغْشَى ‏ ‏امْرَأَتَهُ حَائِضًا ‏
en: Yunus narrated to us, Hammad ibn Salamah narrated to us, from 'Ata' al-'Attar, from 'Ikrimah, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "He should give a dinar in charity; if he cannot find (a dinar), then half a dinar" — meaning the one who has intercourse with his wife while she is menstruating.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يُونُسُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو عَوَانَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سِمَاكٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏لَقِيَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَاعِزَ بْنَ مَالِكٍ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ أَحَقٌّ مَا بَلَغَنِي عَنْكَ قَالَ وَمَا بَلَغَكَ عَنِّي قَالَ ‏ ‏بَلَغَنِي أَنَّكَ ‏ ‏فَجَرْتَ ‏ ‏بِأَمَةِ آلِ فُلَانٍ قَالَ نَعَمْ فَرَدَّهُ حَتَّى شَهِدَ أَرْبَعَ مَرَّاتٍ ثُمَّ أَمَرَ ‏ ‏بِرَجْمِهِ ‏
en: Yunus narrated to us, Abu 'Awanah narrated to us, from Simak, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ met Ma'iz ibn Malik and said: "Is it true what has reached me about you?" He said: "What has reached you about me?" He said: "I have been told that you committed adultery with the slave girl of such-and-such family." He said: "Yes." He sent him back until he testified four times, then he ordered his stoning.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يُونُسُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادٌ يَعْنِي ابْنَ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَلِىِّ بْنِ زَيْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يُوسُفَ بْنِ مِهْرَانَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏جِبْرِيلَ ‏ ‏عَلَيْهِ السَّلَام ‏ ‏قَالَ لِلنَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لَوْ رَأَيْتَنِي وَأَنَا آخُذُ مِنْ ‏ ‏حَالِ ‏ ‏الْبَحْرِ ‏ ‏فَأَدُسُّهُ ‏ ‏فِي ‏ ‏فِي ‏ ‏فِرْعَوْنَ ‏
en: Yunus narrated to us, Hammad, meaning Ibn Salamah, narrated to us, from 'Ali ibn Zayd, from Yusuf ibn Mihran, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), that Jibril (عليه السلام) said to the Prophet ﷺ: "If only you had seen me taking from the mud of the sea and stuffing it into the mouth of Fir'awn."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يُونُسُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادٌ يَعْنِي ابْنَ زَيْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَيُّوبَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏بَعَثَنِي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي ‏ ‏الثَّقَلِ ‏ ‏مِنْ ‏ ‏جَمْعٍ ‏ ‏بِلَيْلٍ ‏
en: Yunus narrated to us, Hammad, meaning Ibn Zayd, narrated to us, from Ayyub, from 'Ikrimah, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent me ahead with the baggage from Muzdalifah (Jam') at night.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يُونُسُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏حَمَّادٍ يَعْنِي ابْنَ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَلِيِّ بْنِ زَيْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يُوسُفَ بْنِ مِهْرَانَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ لِي ‏ ‏جِبْرِيلُ ‏ ‏عَلَيْهِ السَّلَام ‏ ‏إِنَّهُ قَدْ حُبِّبَ إِلَيْكَ الصَّلَاةُ فَخُذْ مِنْهَا مَا شِئْتَ ‏
en: Yunus narrated to us, from Hammad, meaning Ibn Salamah, from 'Ali ibn Zayd, from Yusuf ibn Mihran, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Jibril (عليه السلام) said to me: 'Prayer has been made beloved to you, so take from it as much as you wish.'"

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يُونُسُ ‏ ‏وَعَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادٌ يَعْنِي ابْنَ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَلِيِّ بْنِ زَيْدٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ زَيْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يُوسُفَ بْنِ مِهْرَانَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَجُلًا أَتَى ‏ ‏عُمَرَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ امْرَأَةٌ جَاءَتْ ‏ ‏تُبَايِعُهُ ‏ ‏فَأَدْخَلْتُهَا ‏ ‏الدَّوْلَجَ ‏ ‏فَأَصَبْتُ مِنْهَا مَا دُونَ الْجِمَاعِ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏وَيْحَكَ ‏ ‏لَعَلَّهَا ‏ ‏مُغِيبٌ ‏ ‏فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ قَالَ أَجَلْ قَالَ فَأْتِ ‏ ‏أَبَا بَكْرٍ ‏ ‏فَاسْأَلْهُ قَالَ فَأَتَاهُ فَسَأَلَهُ فَقَالَ لَعَلَّهَا ‏ ‏مُغِيبٌ ‏ ‏فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ قَالَ فَقَالَ مِثْلَ قَوْلِ ‏ ‏عُمَرَ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ أَتَى النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ لَهُ مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ قَالَ فَلَعَلَّهَا ‏ ‏مُغِيبٌ ‏ ‏فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَنَزَلَ الْقُرْآنُ ‏ { ‏وَأَقِمْ الصَّلَاةَ طَرَفَيْ النَّهَارِ ‏ ‏وَزُلَفًا ‏ ‏مِنْ اللَّيْلِ إِنَّ الْحَسَنَاتِ يُذْهِبْنَ السَّيِّئَاتِ ‏} ‏إِلَى آخِرِ الْآيَةِ فَقَالَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ أَلِي خَاصَّةً أَمْ لِلنَّاسِ عَامَّةً فَضَرَبَ ‏ ‏عُمَرُ ‏ ‏صَدْرَهُ بِيَدِهِ فَقَالَ لَا وَلَا نَعْمَةَ عَيْنٍ بَلْ لِلنَّاسِ عَامَّةً فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏صَدَقَ ‏ ‏عُمَرُ ‏
en: Yunus and 'Affan narrated to us, Hammad, meaning Ibn Salamah, narrated to us, from 'Ali ibn Zayd. 'Affan said: 'Ali ibn Zayd informed us, from Yusuf ibn Mihran, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), that a man came to 'Umar (رضي الله عنه) and said: "A woman came to pledge allegiance, so I brought her into a side room and did everything with her short of intercourse." He said: "Woe to you! Perhaps her husband is away in the path of Allah?" He said: "Indeed." He said: "Go to Abu Bakr and ask him." So he went to Abu Bakr and asked him, and Abu Bakr said: "Perhaps her husband is away in the path of Allah?" He said the same as 'Umar. Then the man went to the Prophet ﷺ and told him the same. He said: "Perhaps her husband is away in the path of Allah?" Then the Quran was revealed: {And establish prayer at the two ends of the day and at the approach of the night. Indeed, good deeds drive away evil deeds} [11:114] to the end of the verse. The man said: "O Messenger of Allah, is this specifically for me or for all people generally?" 'Umar struck his chest with his hand and said: "No, and no delight of the eye! Rather, it is for all people generally." The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "'Umar has spoken the truth."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يُونُسُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادٌ يَعْنِي ابْنَ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَلِيِّ بْنِ زَيْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يُوسُفَ بْنِ مِهْرَانَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏جَاءَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَرَدِيفُهُ ‏ ‏أُسَامَةُ بْنُ زَيْدٍ ‏ ‏فَسَقَيْنَاهُ مِنْ هَذَا الشَّرَابِ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏أَحْسَنْتُمْ هَكَذَا فَاصْنَعُوا ‏
en: Yunus narrated to us, Hammad, meaning Ibn Salamah, narrated to us, from 'Ali ibn Zayd, from Yusuf ibn Mihran, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ came with Usamah ibn Zayd riding behind him, and we gave him some of this drink. He said: "You have done well; continue to do this."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مَرْوَانُ بْنُ شُجَاعٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏مَا أَحْفَظُهُ إِلَّا ‏ ‏سَالِمٌ الْأَفْطَسُ الْجَزَرِيُّ ابْنُ عَجْلَانَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي عَنْ ‏ ‏سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏الشِّفَاءُ فِي ثَلَاثَةٍ شَرْبَةِ عَسَلٍ وَشَرْطَةِ ‏ ‏مِحْجَمٍ ‏ ‏وَكَيَّةِ نَارٍ وَأَنْهَى أُمَّتِي عَنْ الْكَيِّ ‏
en: Marwan ibn Shuja' narrated to us, who said: As I recall, it was Salim al-Aftas al-Jazari Ibn 'Ajlan who narrated to me, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما). The Prophet ﷺ said: "The cure is in three things: a drink of honey, the incision of a cupper, and cauterization with fire, but I forbid my Ummah from cauterization."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ عِيسَى ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏إِبْرَاهِيمُ يَعْنِي ابْنَ سَعْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الزُّهْرِيِّ ‏ ‏وَيَعْقُوبُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ شِهَابٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كَانَ الْمُشْرِكُونَ يَفْرُقُونَ رُءُوسَهُمْ وَكَانَ ‏ ‏أَهْلُ الْكِتَابِ ‏ ‏يَسْدِلُونَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏يَعْقُوبُ ‏ ‏أَشْعَارَهُمْ ‏ ‏وَكَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يُحِبُّ وَيُعْجِبُهُ مُوَافَقَةُ ‏ ‏أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏يَعْقُوبُ ‏ ‏فِي بَعْضِ مَا لَمْ يُؤْمَرْ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏إِسْحَاقُ ‏ ‏فِيمَا لَمْ يُؤْمَرْ فِيهِ ‏ ‏فَسَدَلَ ‏ ‏نَاصِيَتَهُ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ ‏ ‏فَرَقَ ‏ ‏بَعْدُ ‏
en: Ishaq ibn 'Isa narrated to us, Ibrahim, meaning Ibn Sa'd, narrated to me, from al-Zuhri. And Ya'qub said: my father narrated to me, from Ibn Shihab, from 'Ubaydullah ibn 'Abdullah, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: The polytheists used to part their hair, while the People of the Book used to let their hair hang down. Ya'qub said: their hair. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ liked and preferred to follow the People of the Book. Ya'qub said: in matters about which he had not been commanded. Ishaq said: in what he had not been commanded regarding. So he let his forelock hang down, then he parted it afterward.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَسَنُ بْنُ مُوسَى ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو خَيْثَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ بْنِ خُثَيْمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي الطُّفَيْلِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏رَأَيْتُ ‏ ‏مُعَاوِيَةَ ‏ ‏يَطُوفُ ‏ ‏بِالْبَيْتِ ‏ ‏عَنْ يَسَارِهِ ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏وَأَنَا ‏ ‏أَتْلُوهُمَا فِي ظُهُورِهِمَا أَسْمَعُ كَلَامَهُمَا ‏ ‏فَطَفِقَ ‏ ‏مُعَاوِيَةُ ‏ ‏يَسْتَلِمُ ‏ ‏رُكْنَ الْحَجَرَ فَقَالَ لَهُ ‏ ‏ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏إِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لَمْ ‏ ‏يَسْتَلِمْ ‏ ‏هَذَيْنِ الرُّكْنَيْنِ فَيَقُولُ ‏ ‏مُعَاوِيَةُ ‏ ‏دَعْنِي مِنْكَ يَا ‏ ‏ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏فَإِنَّهُ لَيْسَ مِنْهَا شَيْءٌ مَهْجُورٌ ‏ ‏فَطَفِقَ ‏ ‏ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏لَا يَزِيدُهُ كُلَّمَا وَضَعَ يَدَهُ عَلَى شَيْءٍ مِنْ الرُّكْنَيْنِ قَالَ لَهُ ذَلِكَ ‏
en: Hasan ibn Musa narrated to us, Abu Khaythamah narrated to us, from 'Abdullah ibn 'Uthman ibn Khuthaym, from Abu al-Tufayl, who said: I saw Mu'awiyah performing tawaf around the House, with 'Abdullah ibn 'Abbas on his left side, and I was following behind them, hearing their speech. Mu'awiyah kept touching the corner of the Black Stone (and the other corners). Ibn 'Abbas said to him: "Indeed, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not touch these two corners (the Shami and Iraqi corners)." Mu'awiyah would say: "Leave me alone, O Ibn 'Abbas, for none of it (the Ka'bah) should be abandoned." But Ibn 'Abbas would not cease; every time Mu'awiyah placed his hand on one of those two corners, he would say the same to him.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يُونُسُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏دَاوُدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ دِينَارٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏اعْتَمَرَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَرْبَعًا عُمْرَةً مِنْ ‏ ‏الْحُدَيْبِيَةِ ‏ ‏وَعُمْرَةَ الْقَضَاءِ فِي ذِي الْقَعْدَةِ مِنْ ‏ ‏قَابِلٍ ‏ ‏وَعُمْرَةَ الثَّالِثَةِ مِنْ ‏ ‏الْجِعِرَّانَةِ ‏ ‏وَالرَّابِعَةَ الَّتِي مَعَ حَجَّتِهِ ‏
en: Yunus narrated to us, Dawud ibn 'Abd al-Rahman narrated to us, from 'Amr ibn Dinar, from 'Ikrimah, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: The Prophet ﷺ performed 'Umrah four times: an 'Umrah from al-Hudaybiyah, the 'Umrah of al-Qada' in Dhul-Qi'dah of the following year, the third 'Umrah from al-Ji'ranah, and the fourth which was with his Hajj.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ أَبِي الْعَبَّاسِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ أَبِي الزِّنَادِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُتْبَةَ بْنِ مَسْعُودٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ أَنْزَلَ ‏ { ‏وَمَنْ لَمْ يَحْكُمْ بِمَا أَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ فَأُولَئِكَ هُمْ الْكَافِرُونَ ‏} ‏وَ ‏ { ‏أُولَئِكَ هُمْ الظَّالِمُونَ ‏} ‏وَ ‏ { ‏أُولَئِكَ هُمْ الْفَاسِقُونَ ‏} ‏قَالَ قَالَ ‏ ‏ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏أَنْزَلَهَا اللَّهُ فِي الطَّائِفَتَيْنِ مِنْ ‏ ‏الْيَهُودِ ‏ ‏وَكَانَتْ إِحْدَاهُمَا قَدْ قَهَرَتْ الْأُخْرَى فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ حَتَّى ارْتَضَوْا ‏ ‏أَوْ اصْطَلَحُوا ‏ ‏عَلَى أَنَّ كُلَّ قَتِيلٍ قَتَلَهُ الْعَزِيزَةُ مِنْ الذَّلِيلَةِ ‏ ‏فَدِيَتُهُ ‏ ‏خَمْسُونَ ‏ ‏وَسْقًا ‏ ‏وَكُلَّ قَتِيلٍ قَتَلَهُ الذَّلِيلَةُ مِنْ الْعَزِيزَةِ ‏ ‏فَدِيَتُهُ ‏ ‏مِائَةُ ‏ ‏وَسْقٍ ‏ ‏فَكَانُوا عَلَى ذَلِكَ حَتَّى قَدِمَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏الْمَدِينَةَ ‏ ‏فَذَلَّتْ الطَّائِفَتَانِ كِلْتَاهُمَا لِمَقْدَمِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَيَوْمَئِذٍ لَمْ يَظْهَرْ وَلَمْ ‏ ‏يُوطِئْهُمَا ‏ ‏عَلَيْهِ وَهُوَ فِي الصُّلْحِ فَقَتَلَتْ الذَّلِيلَةُ مِنْ الْعَزِيزَةِ قَتِيلًا فَأَرْسَلَتْ الْعَزِيزَةُ إِلَى الذَّلِيلَةِ أَنْ ابْعَثُوا إِلَيْنَا بِمِائَةِ ‏ ‏وَسْقٍ ‏ ‏فَقَالَتْ الذَّلِيلَةُ وَهَلْ كَانَ هَذَا فِي حَيَّيْنِ قَطُّ دِينُهُمَا وَاحِدٌ وَنَسَبُهُمَا وَاحِدٌ وَبَلَدُهُمَا وَاحِدٌ ‏ ‏دِيَةُ ‏ ‏بَعْضِهِمْ نِصْفُ ‏ ‏دِيَةِ ‏ ‏بَعْضٍ إِنَّا إِنَّمَا أَعْطَيْنَاكُمْ هَذَا ‏ ‏ضَيْمًا ‏ ‏مِنْكُمْ لَنَا وَفَرَقًا مِنْكُمْ فَأَمَّا إِذْ قَدِمَ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدٌ ‏ ‏فَلَا ‏ ‏نُعْطِيكُمْ ذَلِكَ فَكَادَتْ الْحَرْبُ تَهِيجُ بَيْنَهُمَا ثُمَّ ارْتَضَوْا عَلَى أَنْ يَجْعَلُوا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏بَيْنَهُمْ ثُمَّ ذَكَرَتْ الْعَزِيزَةُ فَقَالَتْ وَاللَّهِ مَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدٌ ‏ ‏بِمُعْطِيكُمْ مِنْهُمْ ضِعْفَ مَا ‏ ‏يُعْطِيهِمْ مِنْكُمْ وَلَقَدْ صَدَقُوا مَا أَعْطَوْنَا هَذَا إِلَّا ‏ ‏ضَيْمًا ‏ ‏مِنَّا وَقَهْرًا لَهُمْ فَدُسُّوا إِلَى ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏مَنْ يَخْبُرُ لَكُمْ رَأْيَهُ إِنْ أَعْطَاكُمْ مَا تُرِيدُونَ حَكَّمْتُمُوهُ وَإِنْ لَمْ يُعْطِكُمْ حَذِرْتُمْ فَلَمْ تُحَكِّمُوهُ فَدَسُّوا إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏نَاسًا مِنْ الْمُنَافِقِينَ لِيَخْبُرُوا لَهُمْ رَأْيَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَلَمَّا جَاءَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَ اللَّهُ رَسُولَهُ بِأَمْرِهِمْ كُلِّهِ وَمَا أَرَادُوا فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ ‏ { ‏يَا أَيُّهَا الرَّسُولُ لَا يَحْزُنْكَ الَّذِينَ يُسَارِعُونَ فِي الْكُفْرِ مِنْ الَّذِينَ قَالُوا آمَنَّا ‏ ‏إِلَى قَوْلِهِ ‏ ‏وَمَنْ لَمْ يَحْكُمْ بِمَا أَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ فَأُولَئِكَ هُمْ الْفَاسِقُونَ ‏} ‏ثُمَّ قَالَ فِيهِمَا وَاللَّهِ نَزَلَتْ وَإِيَّاهُمَا عَنَى اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ ‏
en: Ibrahim ibn Abi al-'Abbas narrated to us, 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi al-Zinad narrated to us, from his father, from 'Ubaydullah ibn 'Abdullah ibn 'Utbah ibn Mas'ud, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: Indeed Allah عز وجل revealed: {And whoever does not judge by what Allah has revealed, those are the disbelievers} [5:44], and {those are the wrongdoers} [5:45], and {those are the defiantly disobedient} [5:47]. He said: Ibn 'Abbas said: Allah revealed these verses regarding the two groups of Jews. One of them had overpowered the other in the pre-Islamic period until they agreed — or made a settlement — that for every person the powerful tribe killed from the weaker tribe, the blood money would be fifty wasq (loads of grain), and for every person the weaker tribe killed from the powerful tribe, the blood money would be one hundred wasq. They remained upon this until the Prophet ﷺ came to Madinah, and both groups were humbled by the arrival of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. At that time he had not yet asserted authority or made them submit to it, as he was in a truce. Then the weaker tribe killed a man from the powerful tribe. The powerful tribe sent word to the weaker tribe: "Send us one hundred wasq." The weaker tribe said: "Has this ever existed between two clans whose religion is the same, their lineage is the same, and their land is the same — that the blood money of some of them is half the blood money of others? We only gave you this out of your oppression of us and our fear of you. But now that Muhammad has arrived, we will not give you that." War nearly erupted between them, then they agreed to make the Messenger of Allah ﷺ judge between them. Then the powerful tribe thought and said: "By Allah, Muhammad will not give you double from them what he gives them from you. They spoke truly — they only gave us this out of our oppression and dominance over them. So send someone to Muhammad to find out his opinion. If he gives you what you want, accept him as judge; if not, be wary and do not accept him." So they sent some hypocrites to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ to find out his opinion. When the matter came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, Allah informed His Messenger of all their affairs and what they intended. So Allah عز وجل revealed: {O Messenger, let not those who hasten into disbelief grieve you, of those who say 'We believe'...} to His saying: {And whoever does not judge by what Allah has revealed, those are the defiantly disobedient} [5:41-47]. Then he said: By Allah, these verses were revealed regarding them, and it was them whom Allah عز وجل meant.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَاصِمٍ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏خَالِدٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَنْ يَسْتَمِعْ إِلَى حَدِيثِ قَوْمٍ وَهُمْ لَهُ كَارِهُونَ صُبَّ فِي أُذُنِهِ ‏ ‏الْآنُكُ ‏ ‏وَمَنْ ‏ ‏تَحَلَّمَ ‏ ‏عُذِّبَ حَتَّى ‏ ‏يَعْقِدَ ‏ ‏شَعِيرَةً وَلَيْسَ بِعَاقِدٍ وَمَنْ صَوَّرَ صُورَةً كُلِّفَ أَنْ يَنْفُخَ فِيهَا وَلَيْسَ بِنَافِخٍ ‏
en: 'Ali ibn 'Asim narrated to us, Khalid informed us, from 'Ikrimah, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Whoever eavesdrops on the conversation of a people who dislike it will have molten lead poured into his ears. Whoever claims to have seen a dream (that he did not see) will be punished until he ties a knot in a barley grain, and he will never be able to do so. And whoever makes an image will be tasked to blow the soul into it, and he will never be able to do so."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَاصِمٍ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمْرِو بْنِ غَلَابٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْحَكَمِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْأَعْرَجِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كُنْتُ عِنْدَ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏فِي بَيْتِ ‏ ‏السِّقَايَةِ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ ‏ ‏مُتَوَسِّدٌ ‏ ‏بُرْدًا ‏ ‏لَهُ قَالَ فَقُلْتُ يَا ‏ ‏أَبَا عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏أَخْبِرْنِي عَنْ ‏ ‏عَاشُورَاءَ ‏ ‏قَالَ عَنْ أَيِّ ‏ ‏بَالِهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ قُلْتُ عَنْ صِيَامِهِ قَالَ ‏ ‏إِذَا أَنْتَ ‏ ‏أَهْلَلْتَ ‏ ‏الْمُحَرَّمَ فَاعْدُدْ تِسْعًا ثُمَّ أَصْبِحْ يَوْمَ التَّاسِعِ صَائِمًا قُلْتُ كَذَا كَانَ يَصُومُهُ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدٌ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ نَعَمْ ‏
en: 'Ali ibn 'Asim narrated to us, Mu'awiyah ibn 'Amr ibn Ghalab informed us, from al-Hakam ibn 'Abdullah ibn al-A'raj, who said: I was with Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما) in the house of al-Siqayah (the watering station), and he was reclining on a garment of his. I said: "O Abu 'Abbas, tell me about 'Ashura." He said: "About which aspect of it?" I said: "About its fast." He said: "When you see the crescent of Muharram, count nine, then wake on the ninth day fasting." I said: "Is that how Muhammad ﷺ used to fast it?" He said: "Yes."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَاصِمٍ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنِي ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عُثْمَانَ بْنِ خُثَيْمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَأْتِي هَذَا الْحَجَرُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ لَهُ عَيْنَانِ يُبْصِرُ بِهِمَا وَلِسَانٌ يَنْطِقُ بِهِ يَشْهَدُ لِمَنْ ‏ ‏اسْتَلَمَهُ ‏ ‏بِحَقٍّ ‏
en: 'Ali ibn 'Asim narrated to us, 'Abdullah ibn 'Uthman ibn Khuthaym informed me, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "This Stone (the Black Stone) will come on the Day of Judgment with two eyes with which it sees and a tongue with which it speaks, testifying in favor of whoever touched it sincerely."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَاصِمٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ قَالَ ‏ ‏دَاوُدُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةُ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كَانَ نَاسٌ مِنْ الْأَسْرَى يَوْمَ ‏ ‏بَدْرٍ ‏ ‏لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُمْ فِدَاءٌ ‏ ‏فَجَعَلَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِدَاءَهُمْ أَنْ يُعَلِّمُوا أَوْلَادَ ‏ ‏الْأَنْصَارِ ‏ ‏الْكِتَابَةَ قَالَ فَجَاءَ يَوْمًا غُلَامٌ يَبْكِي إِلَى أَبِيهِ فَقَالَ مَا شَأْنُكَ قَالَ ضَرَبَنِي مُعَلِّمِي قَالَ الْخَبِيثُ يَطْلُبُ ‏ ‏بِذَحْلِ ‏ ‏بَدْرٍ ‏ ‏وَاللَّهِ لَا تَأْتِيهِ أَبَدًا ‏
en: 'Ali ibn 'Asim narrated to us, who said: Dawud said: 'Ikrimah narrated to us, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: Some of the captives on the Day of Badr had no ransom, so the Messenger of Allah ﷺ made their ransom that they teach the children of the Ansar how to write. He said: One day a boy came crying to his father. He said: "What is the matter?" He said: "My teacher hit me." The father said: "The wretch! Seeking revenge for (the defeat at) Badr! By Allah, you will never go to him again."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَاصِمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَطَاءِ بْنِ السَّائِبِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَمَرَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَوْمَ ‏ ‏أُحُدٍ ‏ ‏بِالشُّهَدَاءِ أَنْ يُنْزَعَ عَنْهُمْ الْحَدِيدُ وَالْجُلُودُ وَقَالَ ادْفِنُوهُمْ بِدِمَائِهِمْ وَثِيَابِهِمْ ‏
en: 'Ali ibn 'Asim narrated to us, from 'Ata' ibn al-Sa'ib, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ ordered on the Day of Uhud that the iron (armor) and leather be removed from the martyrs, and he said: "Bury them with their blood and their clothes."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَاصِمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏دَاوُدَ بْنِ أَبِي هِنْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَجُلًا مِنْ ‏ ‏الْأَنْصَارِ ‏ ‏ارْتَدَّ عَنْ الْإِسْلَامِ وَلَحِقَ بِالْمُشْرِكِينَ فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى ‏ { ‏كَيْفَ يَهْدِي اللَّهُ قَوْمًا كَفَرُوا بَعْدَ إِيمَانِهِمْ ‏} ‏إِلَى آخِرِ الْآيَةِ فَبَعَثَ بِهَا قَوْمُهُ فَرَجَعَ تَائِبًا فَقَبِلَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏ذَلِكَ مِنْهُ وَخَلَّى عَنْهُ ‏
en: 'Ali ibn 'Asim narrated to us, from Dawud ibn Abi Hind, from 'Ikrimah, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), that a man from the Ansar apostatized from Islam and joined the polytheists. Then Allah Most High revealed: {How shall Allah guide a people who disbelieved after their belief?} [3:86] to the end of the verse. His people sent it to him, and he returned repentant. The Prophet ﷺ accepted that from him and let him go.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيٌّ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عُثْمَانَ بْنِ خُثَيْمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏الْبَسُوا مِنْ ثِيَابِكُمْ الْبَيَاضَ فَإِنَّهَا مِنْ خَيْرِ ثِيَابِكُمْ وَكَفِّنُوا فِيهَا مَوْتَاكُمْ وَإِنَّ مِنْ خَيْرِ ‏ ‏أَكْحَالِكُمُ ‏ ‏الْإِثْمِدَ ‏ ‏يَجْلُو ‏ ‏الْبَصَرَ وَيُنْبِتُ الشَّعَرَ ‏
en: 'Ali said: 'Abdullah ibn 'Uthman ibn Khuthaym informed us, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Wear white from among your garments, for it is among the best of your garments, and shroud your dead in it. And among the best of your kohl is ithmid (antimony); it clears the sight and causes hair to grow."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَاصِمٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْجُرَيْرِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي الطُّفَيْلِ ‏ ‏وَعَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ بْنِ خُثَيْمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي الطُّفَيْلِ ‏ ‏كِلَاهُمَا ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏رَمَلَ ‏ ‏رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏ثَلَاثَةَ أَشْوَاطٍ ‏ ‏بِالْبَيْتِ ‏ ‏إِذَا انْتَهَى إِلَى الرُّكْنِ الْيَمَانِي وَمَشَى حَتَّى يَأْتِيَ الْحَجَرَ ثُمَّ ‏ ‏يَرْمُلُ ‏ ‏وَمَشَى أَرْبَعَةَ أَطْوَافٍ قَالَ قَالَ ‏ ‏ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏وَكَانَتْ سُنَّةً ‏
en: 'Ali ibn 'Asim narrated to us, from al-Jurayri and 'Abdullah ibn 'Uthman ibn Khuthaym, from Abu al-Tufayl, both from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed ramal (brisk walking) for three circuits around the House. When he reached the Yemeni corner, he would walk until he came to the (Black) Stone, then resume ramal. He walked (normally) for four circuits. He said: Ibn 'Abbas said: And that was a sunnah.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَاصِمٍ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏الْحَذَّاءُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏بَرَكَةَ أَبِي الْوَلِيدِ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَاعِدًا فِي ‏ ‏الْمَسْجِدِ ‏ ‏مُسْتَقْبِلًا الْحُجَرَ قَالَ فَنَظَرَ إِلَى السَّمَاءِ فَضَحِكَ ثُمَّ قَالَ ‏ ‏لَعَنَ اللَّهُ ‏ ‏الْيَهُودَ ‏ ‏حُرِّمَتْ عَلَيْهِمْ الشُّحُومُ فَبَاعُوهَا وَأَكَلُوا أَثْمَانَهَا وَإِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ إِذَا حَرَّمَ عَلَى قَوْمٍ أَكْلَ شَيْءٍ حَرَّمَ عَلَيْهِمْ ثَمَنَهُ ‏
en: 'Ali ibn 'Asim narrated to us, al-Hadhdha' informed us, from Barakah Abu al-Walid, Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما) informed us, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ was sitting in the mosque facing the chambers (of his wives). He looked up at the sky and laughed, then said: "May Allah curse the Jews. Animal fat was forbidden to them, so they sold it and consumed its price. Indeed, when Allah عز وجل forbids a people from consuming something, He also forbids them from consuming its price."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَاصِمٍ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو الْمُعَلَّى الْعَطَّارُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْحَسَنُ الْعُرَنِيُّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏ذُكِرَ عِنْدَ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏يَقْطَعُ الصَّلَاةَ الْكَلْبُ وَالْحِمَارُ وَالْمَرْأَةُ قَالَ بِئْسَمَا عَدَلْتُمْ بِامْرَأَةٍ مُسْلِمَةٍ كَلْبًا وَحِمَارًا لَقَدْ رَأَيْتُنِي ‏ ‏أَقْبَلْتُ عَلَى حِمَارٍ وَرَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يُصَلِّي بِالنَّاسِ حَتَّى إِذَا كُنْتُ قَرِيبًا مِنْهُ مُسْتَقْبِلَهُ نَزَلْتُ عَنْهُ وَخَلَّيْتُ عَنْهُ وَدَخَلْتُ مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي صَلَاتِهِ فَمَا أَعَادَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏صَلَاتَهُ وَلَا نَهَانِي عَمَّا صَنَعْتُ وَلَقَدْ كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يُصَلِّي بِالنَّاسِ فَجَاءَتْ ‏ ‏وَلِيدَةٌ ‏ ‏تَخَلَّلُ ‏ ‏الصُّفُوفَ حَتَّى عَاذَتْ بِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَمَا أَعَادَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏صَلَاتَهُ وَلَا نَهَاهَا عَمَّا صَنَعَتْ وَلَقَدْ كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يُصَلِّي فِي مَسْجِدٍ فَخَرَجَ جَدْيٌ مِنْ بَعْضِ حُجُرَاتِ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَذَهَبَ يَجْتَازُ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ فَمَنَعَهُ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏أَفَلَا تَقُولُونَ الْجَدْيُ يَقْطَعُ الصَّلَاةَ ‏
en: 'Ali ibn 'Asim narrated to us, Abu al-Mu'alla al-'Attar informed us, al-Hasan al-'Urani narrated to us, who said: It was mentioned in the presence of Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما) that a dog, a donkey, and a woman invalidate the prayer. He said: "How evil is your comparison of a Muslim woman to a dog and a donkey! I came riding a donkey while the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was leading the people in prayer. When I was close to him, facing him, I dismounted, let the donkey go, and joined the Messenger of Allah ﷺ in his prayer. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not repeat his prayer, nor did he rebuke me for what I did. And the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was once leading the people in prayer when a young slave girl came through the rows until she took refuge with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. He did not repeat his prayer, nor did he rebuke her for what she did. And the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was once praying in a mosque when a young goat came out from one of the Prophet's rooms and tried to pass in front of him. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ prevented it." Ibn 'Abbas said: "Then why don't you say a goat invalidates the prayer?"

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مَيْمُونٍ أَبُو عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الرَّقِّيُّ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏الْحَسَنُ يَعْنِي أَبَا الْمَلِيحِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏حَبِيبٍ يَعْنِي ابْنَ أَبِي مَرْزُوقٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏مَنْ قَدِمَ حَاجًّا وَطَافَ ‏ ‏بِالْبَيْتِ ‏ ‏وَبَيْنَ ‏ ‏الصَّفَا ‏ ‏وَالْمَرْوَةِ ‏ ‏فَقَدْ انْقَضَتْ ‏ ‏حَجَّتُهُ وَصَارَتْ عُمْرَةً كَذَلِكَ سُنَّةُ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ وَسُنَّةُ رَسُولِهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏
en: 'Abdullah ibn Maymun Abu 'Abd al-Rahman al-Raqqi narrated to us, who said: al-Hasan, meaning Abu al-Malih, informed us, from Habib, meaning Ibn Abi Marzuq, from 'Ata', from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), who said: Whoever comes as a pilgrim and performs tawaf of the House and (sa'i) between al-Safa and al-Marwah, then his Hajj is concluded and it has become an 'Umrah. Such is the Sunnah of Allah عز وجل and the Sunnah of His Messenger ﷺ.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏زَيْدُ بْنُ الْحُبَابِ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏سَيْفٌ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏قَيْسُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ الْمَكِّيُّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ دِينَارٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَضَى بِشَاهِدٍ وَيَمِينٍ ‏
en: Zayd ibn al-Hubab narrated to us, Sayf informed us, Qays ibn Sa'd al-Makki informed us, from 'Amr ibn Dinar, from Ibn 'Abbas (رضي الله عنهما), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ judged based on one witness and an oath.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ الرَّقِّيُّ أَبُو يَزِيدَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏فُرَاتٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الْكَرِيمِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَبُو جَهْلٍ ‏ ‏لَئِنْ رَأَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يُصَلِّي عِنْدَ ‏ ‏الْكَعْبَةِ ‏ ‏لَآتِيَنَّهُ حَتَّى ‏ ‏أَطَأَ ‏ ‏عَلَى عُنُقِهِ قَالَ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏لَوْ فَعَلَ لَأَخَذَتْهُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ عِيَانًا وَلَوْ أَنَّ ‏ ‏الْيَهُودَ ‏ ‏تَمَنَّوْا الْمَوْتَ لَمَاتُوا وَرَأَوْا مَقَاعِدَهُمْ فِي النَّارِ وَلَوْ خَرَجَ الَّذِينَ ‏ ‏يُبَاهِلُونَ ‏ ‏رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لَرَجَعُوا لَا يَجِدُونَ مَالًا وَلَا أَهْلًا ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَحْمَدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عُبَيْدُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الْكَرِيمِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَبُو جَهْلٍ ‏ ‏فَذَكَرَ مَعْنَاهُ ‏
